dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: This outfit has all the elegance and appeal of a 1980s women’s power suit. The short spencer with wide lapels, the strong (albeit not boxy) shoulders, the slimfit T-shirt and knee-skimming skirt capture the concept to perfection. By contrast, what creates the amazing sense of magic and uniqueness is the material mix – a sum total of lusciously light silk, lace & tone-on- tone embroidery – with the quietly sunny monochrome straw hue heightening the impact in the finest of ways. The oneiric quality of the look culminates in the stunning floral ornamention at front waist. In the final analysis, this latter element becomes the true focal point of the ensemble, in stark opposition to the overall classy simplicity of shape.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Jacket spencer style with one-button single breasting. Extrawide lapels, long slender sleeves with overlay slit cuff, padded bombé shoulders. Skirt with high waist. Straight cut, knee length. T-shirt with low-cut scoop neckline. Short-sleeve slimfit style. Flowers in soft ivory and straw yellow shades of silk organza, silk organza, lace and raffia.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.materials: Straw color tussah silk with partial tone-on-tone raffia embroidery (Forster Willi, Switzerland; art. 99584, col. MM10), nude color silk crêpe de chine (Taroni, Como; art. 16849, art. 3049/bis), floral design ivory silk & gold lurex lace (Hurel, France; art. 78779/2, var. ORPAL)

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: While lines are essentially simple – as in gently flaring tunic and straight knee-skimming skirt – this outfit succeeds in capturing all the aristo allure of once-upon-a-time lingerie, elegantly sensual, exquisitely seductive though not openly sexy. What works the special magic is the delicate bois de rose hue. Yet most of all the trick is in the airy lightness of the fundamental material, the tulle that is taken to the next level on the fascination front thanks to the gorgeous lace appliquéwork, the fine horizontal topstitching, the garland & flower ornamentation, the sublime layering with taffeta.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Tunic A-line style. Scoop neckline, 7/8 length. Construction featuring the hand application of lace, three layers of (different) fabric sewn together with 1 cm parallel topstitching. Skirt with straight line, no waistband. Same type of construction as for tunic. Hat in natural straw. Large size, dome style. Hand sewn. Flowers in silk organza.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.working_process: Lace in appliqué form; parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.materials: Silk lace in lovely bois de rose hue, taffeta with sweet bouquet design, garland & flower decors (Ottolenghi, Milano), bois de rose silk tulle (Zecchini, Milano), black nylon tulle

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: Despite two characteristics contradicting the idea – namely, the lack of a hood and the presence of lavish gigot sleeves – this cloak-duster is ideally a revisitation of the Maghrebian burnus (or alsham). The designer’s intent is definitely there in the luscious shoulder-to-floor draping and in the rich flaring toward the bottom. The trousers capture the same sense of soft fluidity reminiscent of Arabian garb. The chromatic uniformity of the ensemble’s two key pieces enhances and exalts the stylistic coherence, the underlying inspiration too.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Duster in ultra soft and fluid A line, ankle length. Strong shoulders, magnificent puff sleeves with silk organza layer in matching color. Pants in coral cady. Extrawide leg bottom, deep unpressed pleat. Semi-hidden pockets. Belt with fabric covering, rectangular gold chrome buckle. Top brassière style in coral cady with adhesive element. Hat in natural straw, color black. Large size, dome style. Hand sewn.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.working_process: Openwork (ajour embroidery) edging
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.materials: Coral color silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691, col. 4002), silk organza (Taroni, Como)

Pants in coral cady. Extrawide leg bottom, deep unpressed pleat. Semi-hidden pockets. Belt with fabric covering, rectangular gold chrome buckle. Top brassière style in coral cady with adhesive element. Hat in natural straw, color black. Large size, dome style. Hand sewn.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.working_process: Openwork (ajour embroidery) edging
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.materials: Coral color silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691, col. 4002), silk organza (Taroni, Como)

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: Despite two characteristics contradicting the idea – namely, the lack of a hood and the presence of lavish gigot sleeves – this cloak-duster is ideally a revisitation of the Maghrebian burnus (or alsham). The designer’s intent is definitely there in the luscious shoulder-to-floor draping and in the rich flaring toward the bottom. The trousers capture the same sense of soft fluidity reminiscent of Arabian garb. The chromatic uniformity of the ensemble’s two key pieces enhances and exalts the stylistic coherence, the underlying inspiration too.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: While stopping well above the knee, this jacket captures all the flair of the magnificent mid 19th century evening cloaks (1830s to ‘50s) typically complementing full-length body-skimming fourreaux. What both the original versions and Ferré’s revisitation have in common are two primary features: firstly, the gorgeous fabric (here a fabulous silk marocain) that gives structure and emphasis to a design basically simple of construction; and secondly, the lavish beauty of the soft puff sleeves. Equally important in creating the sense of richness is the intentional decorative complexity and utter sophistication of the print motif, namely Vienna-strawlike rosettes making a stunning impression on a black background and then intensifying of intricacy on the sleeves (in a twofold manner: plain tone for one part, paisley pattern for the other).
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Jacket in 7/8 length with straight line, round neck. Rich puff sleeves with silk organza layer on the inside. Skirt in silk cady. Straight line with narrow waistband. Floor length, back slit. Top debardeur style in silk cady. Blousy effect, hip-hugging basque. Belt in Vienna straw, with matching rectangular leather buckle.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.bibliography: “Gianfranco Ferré. I quaderni di Pitti 01”, Giunti Editore, Firenze, 2000
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: What sets this ensemble a class apart is the vibrant Mediterranean allure that comes through in the skirt’s strong chromatic impact and graceful ease. Contrasting nicely with the simplicity and severity of the plain black top are the bright colors of the bayadère stripe-type flounces along the base of the long full black tulle outer skirt. The same upbeat palette appears in print form on the short slim crepe underskirt. To complete the sensation of exuberant vitality there’s a sash knotted at the waist. Ever an emblem of fetching Mediterranean femininity, the sash heightens the sinuous grace of swaying hips. Donation to the Pitti Palace Costume Gallery in Florence (2000).
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Evening dress in four pieces: bodice, skirt, petticoat, sash. Bodice in black silk crêpe. Slim fit with narrow straps, back zip/clasp closure. Skirt in black tulle with black/red/pink/yellow/sky blue/green bias-cut silk gazar flounces. Clasp/snap fastening at front waist, opening onto an underskirt. Petticoat in print silk crêpe (same colors as skirt). Slim line, knee length, small back slit, side zip/button closure. Sash in matching print silk organza. To knot at waist. Black crepe lining for bodice. Stiff nylon understructure & black satin lining for skirt. Black crêpe lining for petticoat.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.exhibitions: “Altre Emozioni”, Firenze, 2000; “Moda tra analogie e dissonanze”, Firenze, 2010
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.materials: Silk crepe (Taroni, Como; art. 16691, col. 120), silk gazar (Clerici Tessuto, Como; art. 1234, col. 398, 470, 205, 298, 198, 122, black), print silk crepe (Lucchini, Milano; art. 6621-67, col. 81), print silk organza (Clerici Tessuto, Como; art. 1234, col.9 & Lucchini, Milano; art. 6621, col.74

Contrasting nicely with the simplicity and severity of the plain black top are the bright colors of the bayadère stripe-type flounces along the base of the long full black tulle outer skirt. The same upbeat palette appears in print form on the short slim crepe underskirt. To complete the sensation of exuberant vitality there’s a sash knotted at the waist. Ever an emblem of fetching Mediterranean femininity, the sash heightens the sinuous grace of swaying hips. Donation to the Pitti Palace Costume Gallery in Florence (2000).
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Evening dress in four pieces: bodice, skirt, petticoat, sash. Bodice in black silk crêpe. Slim fit with narrow straps, back zip/clasp closure. Skirt in black tulle with black/red/pink/yellow/sky blue/green bias-cut silk gazar flounces. Clasp/snap fastening at front waist, opening onto an underskirt. Petticoat in print silk crêpe (same colors as skirt). Slim line, knee length, small back slit, side zip/button closure. Sash in matching print silk organza. To knot at waist. Black crepe lining for bodice. Stiff nylon understructure & black satin lining for skirt. Black crêpe lining for petticoat.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: The richness and uniqueness of the ensemble reside entirely in the decoration of the jacket, simple in its construction but incredibly rich in material choices and above all decorations. The baiadera stripes that animate it not only juxtapose colors in stark contrast but also alternate different decorative techniques - from hand embroidery to loom-executed embroidery overlaid on the print - giving not only a sense of opulence to the garment but also of almost three-dimensional material density. Similar, although more measured and confined to the border, is the rich decorative identity of the scarf-shawl, which, wrapped around the neck, seems to cascade carelessly down the back. The severity of the black trousers, also wide and flowing, which once again seems to pay tribute to Middle Eastern tradition, recalibrates the whole ensemble.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.present_location: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Long jacket, round neck, with a slim line; kissed closure up to the waist; elbow-length sleeves. Embroidery with horizontal baiadera stripes. The embroidery motif is composed as follows (from top to bottom). At the top of the shoulder, a black band composed of: 2 rows of jais, 4 rows of beads, 1 row alternating 1 canottiglia and 3 beads, 1 row alternating 3 larger black beads and one gold bead of the same size. This is followed by 1 row of gold beads and 1 row of gold jais. The green section continues with 1 row of jais and 1 row of canottiglie. Another gold section follows: 1 row of canottiglie, 1 row of jais alternating gold/ivory, 1 row of canottiglie. Then, a red section descends from the shoulders to the chest, composed as follows: 1 row of canottiglie, 1 row of beads arranged to form what appears to be a tube with vertical stripes, 1 row of jais of large dimensions, 1 row of large smooth round beads and one of large irregular beads, 1 more row of beads arranged in a tube, 3 rows of irregular beads occasionally interspersed with black beads, 1 row of tube beads alternating 2 rows of red and 2 rows of black, 1 row composed of jais with pendulums resting on paillettes of ivory color, 1 row of beads alternating 1 red and 3 black, 2 rows of red beads, 1 row of large jais. Subsequently, 1 gold section composed of 1 row of wooden beads and 3 rows of decreasing-sized gold beads. 1 green section composed of 1 row of beads, of two different sizes alternating light green and dark green, and 1 row of transparent colored green glass beads. This is followed by a pink section: 1 row of canottiglie, 1 row of colored glass beads, 1 row of canottiglie. This is followed by another green section: 1 row of beads with pendulums resting on paillettes of ivory color, 1 row of square jais, 1 row of round beads. A row of wooden beads closes the chest area. Then, a red section follows: 1 row of round beads, 1 row of square jais, 1 row of beads arranged to form what appears to be a tube with vertical stripes, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 row of beads surrounded by visible metallic support. This is followed by a green section: 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 row of vertical canottiglie from which beads hang resting on paillettes of ivory color, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 row of transparent colored glass beads, 1 row of vertical canottiglie. A repeat row of wooden beads marks the beginning of the waist. 1 row of black beads and 1 row of smaller black beads. 1 pink section composed of: 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 row of jais shaped like a drop, 1 row of transparent glass beads. 3 successive rows of gold beads leading to 1 row of horizontal rectangular red canottiglie. Next, a weave of wooden beads from which green-colored beads hang, starting a new dark green section: jais, 2 rows of beads, 1 row of pearls alternating 2 dark greens and 1 black, 1 row alternating green, pink, and red paillettes. The waist is then marked by a row alternating 3 wooden beads in coral color and 1 green bead. This is followed by a large green section: 1 row of vertical canottiglie, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie from which beads hang resting on paillettes of ivory color, 1 additional row of densely packed vertical canottiglie, 1 row of transparent colored beads. This is followed by a gold section: 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 with a crossed pattern between silver jais, gold beads, and wood. This is followed by a large black section composed of: 1 row of canottiglie, 1 band with a triangular pattern created by gold beads bordered by red beads from which 3 movable red canottiglie hang, 7 rows of black beads, 1 row of horizontal black canottiglie, 3 rows of black beads, 1 with a crossed pattern between purple jais, black beads, and wood, 6 rows of black beads, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie. Next, 4 rows of wooden beads, 2 of green beads, and again 3 of wooden beads. The jacket continues towards the end with a red section: 1 row of square jais, 1 row of pearls, 1 row of beads alternating red and black with pearls that hang and rest on ivory colored sequins 1 row of beads arranged to form a tube
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.exhibitions: “Gianfranco Ferré. Moda, un racconto nei disegni”, Centro Culturale Santa Maria della Pietà, Cremona, 2017
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.working_process: Allover hand loom embroidery
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.materials: Silk organza (Clerici, Como, art 1234 col. 1); transparent colored glass beads; sequins; jet beads; canottiglie; wooden and mother-of-pearl beads; all in the following colors: coral, red, purple, ivory, gold, peacock green, pink, silver, and black

This is followed by a gold section: 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 with a crossed pattern between silver jais, gold beads, and wood. This is followed by a large black section composed of: 1 row of canottiglie, 1 band with a triangular pattern created by gold beads bordered by red beads from which 3 movable red canottiglie hang, 7 rows of black beads, 1 row of horizontal black canottiglie, 3 rows of black beads, 1 with a crossed pattern between purple jais, black beads, and wood, 6 rows of black beads, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie. Next, 4 rows of wooden beads, 2 of green beads, and again 3 of wooden beads. The jacket continues towards the end with a red section: 1 row of square jais, 1 row of pearls, 1 row of beads alternating red and black with pearls that hang and rest on ivory colored sequins 1 row of beads arranged to form a tube

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: The richness and uniqueness of the ensemble reside entirely in the decoration of the jacket, simple in its construction but incredibly rich in material choices and above all decorations. The baiadera stripes that animate it not only juxtapose colors in stark contrast but also alternate different decorative techniques - from hand embroidery to loom-executed embroidery overlaid on the print - giving not only a sense of opulence to the garment but also of almost three-dimensional material density. Similar, although more measured and confined to the border, is the rich decorative identity of the scarf-shawl, which, wrapped around the neck, seems to cascade carelessly down the back. The severity of the black trousers, also wide and flowing, which once again seems to pay tribute to Middle Eastern tradition, recalibrates the whole ensemble.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Long jacket, round neck, with a slim line; kissed closure up to the waist; elbow-length sleeves. Embroidery with horizontal baiadera stripes. The embroidery motif is composed as follows (from top to bottom). This is followed by 1 row of gold beads and 1 row of gold jais. The green section continues with 1 row of jais and 1 row of canottiglie. Another gold section follows: 1 row of canottiglie, 1 row of jais alternating gold/ivory, 1 row of canottiglie. Then, a red section descends from the shoulders to the chest, composed as follows: 1 row of canottiglie, 1 row of beads arranged to form what appears to be a tube with vertical stripes, 1 row of jais of large dimensions, 1 row of large smooth round beads and one of large irregular beads, 1 more row of beads arranged in a tube, 3 rows of irregular beads occasionally interspersed with black beads, 1 row of tube beads alternating 2 rows of red and 2 rows of black, 1 row composed of jais with pendulums resting on paillettes of ivory color, 1 row of beads alternating 1 red and 3 black, 2 rows of red beads, 1 row of large jais. Subsequently, 1 gold section composed of 1 row of wooden beads and 3 rows of decreasing-sized gold beads. 1 green section composed of 1 row of beads, of two different sizes alternating light green and dark green, and 1 row of transparent colored green glass beads. This is followed by a pink section: 1 row of canottiglie, 1 row of colored glass beads, 1 row of canottiglie. This is followed by another green section: 1 row of beads with pendulums resting on paillettes of ivory color, 1 row of square jais, 1 row of round beads. A row of wooden beads closes the chest area. Then, a red section follows: 1 row of round beads, 1 row of square jais, 1 row of beads arranged to form what appears to be a tube with vertical stripes, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 row of beads surrounded by visible metallic support. This is followed by a green section: 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 row of vertical canottiglie from which beads hang resting on paillettes of ivory color, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 row of transparent colored glass beads, 1 row of vertical canottiglie. A repeat row of wooden beads marks the beginning of the waist. 1 row of black beads and 1 row of smaller black beads. 1 pink section composed of: 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 row of jais shaped like a drop, 1 row of transparent glass beads. 3 successive rows of gold beads leading to 1 row of horizontal rectangular red canottiglie. Next, a weave of wooden beads from which green-colored beads hang, starting a new dark green section: jais, 2 rows of beads, 1 row of pearls alternating 2 dark greens and 1 black, 1 row alternating green, pink, and red paillettes. This is followed by a large green section: 1 row of vertical canottiglie, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie from which beads hang resting on paillettes of ivory color, 1 additional row of densely packed vertical canottiglie, 1 row of transparent colored beads. This is followed by a gold section: 1 row of horizontal canottiglie, 1 with a crossed pattern between silver jais, gold beads, and wood. This is followed by a large black section composed of: 1 row of canottiglie, 1 band with a triangular pattern created by gold beads bordered by red beads from which 3 movable red canottiglie hang, 7 rows of black beads, 1 row of horizontal black canottiglie, 3 rows of black beads, 1 with a crossed pattern between purple jais, black beads, and wood, 6 rows of black beads, 1 row of horizontal canottiglie. Next, 4 rows of wooden beads, 2 of green beads, and again 3 of wooden beads. The jacket continues towards the end with a red section: 1 row of square jais, 1 row of pearls, 1 row of beads alternating red and black with pearls that hang and rest on ivory colored sequins 1 row of beads arranged to form a tube

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: La composta asimmetria dei volumi, la cui ricchezza è intenzionalmente tutta spostata lateralmente, il contrasto tra lunghezze, più che misurate nella gonna in sé e decisamente importanti nei pannelli ad inserto, diversi per dimensioni e “raccordati” alla gonna stessa da un “bouquet” floreale multimaterico, segnano l’identità fortemente architettonica, solo in apparenza “scomposta”, della costruzione del capo, senza penalizzarne la lievità, la sensazione forte di movimento che lo percorre anche grazie al gioco di plissettature a vista, né la morbida romantica anima cromatica, solare e rosata al contempo. Riflette questa aerea complessità dell’insieme il body: ridotto ai minimi termini per costruzione, è in verità assolutamente prezioso nella sua definizione decorativa. In questo completo, come in tutta la collezione, il leit motiv è costituito dall’accostamento singolare ed innovativo tra le tipologie più femminili ed aeree della seta e delle materie sostanzialmente estranee agli orizzonti dell’abbigliamento di lusso, in primis paglia di Vienna e rafia, che tuttavia riescono a creare impressioni sfarzo ed opulenza autentici. Secondo capo. il primo usato in sfilata è alla Galleria del Costume di Palazzo Pitti a Firenze ( donazione 2000)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Gonna in pizzo color crema doppiato in tulle e in organza di seta in tinta che avvolge la vita come una cintura; chiusura posteriore con automatici. Sul fianco sinistro sono inseriti due pannelli in pizzo color crema doppiato in tulle, sovrapposti e di dimensioni diverse, con orlo festonato, ripresi, a formare doppio piegone con doppia balza di tulle plissettato alla cintura e organza di seta color rosa e color carne. Sul fianco sinistro, applicazione di rose e ramoscelli in organza e rafia naturale, color grigio perla e rosa cipria. Fodera in organza di seta a righe rosa e color carne. Body in tulle elasticizzato color carne, ricamato con perline in varie tonalità di rosa e argento, foderato in crepe di seta in tinta. Chiusura posteriore con automatici.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.exhibitions: “Moda tra analogie e dissonanze”, Firenze, 2010
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.materials: Organza di seta (Clerici Tessuto,Como; art. 1540/203); tulle di seta (Zecchini, Milano).

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Gonna in pizzo color crema doppiato in tulle e in organza di seta in tinta che avvolge la vita come una cintura; chiusura posteriore con automatici. Sul fianco sinistro sono inseriti due pannelli in pizzo color crema doppiato in tulle, sovrapposti e di dimensioni diverse, con orlo festonato, ripresi, a formare doppio piegone con doppia balza di tulle plissettato alla cintura e organza di seta color rosa e color carne. Sul fianco sinistro, applicazione di rose e ramoscelli in organza e rafia naturale, color grigio perla e rosa cipria. Fodera in organza di seta a righe rosa e color carne. Body in tulle elasticizzato color carne, ricamato con perline in varie tonalità di rosa e argento, foderato in crepe di seta in tinta. Chiusura posteriore con automatici.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: Alla semplicità dell’abito si contrappone lo sfarzo di impronta barocca del caban, con le volute che richiamano i legni incurvati e gli intrecci in stile “Thonet”. L’enfasi è accentuata dalla preziosità delle applicazioni in jais e dai ricami, che contribuiscono a dare un’impressione di femminità sontuosa.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Abito a bustier di linea dritta lungo alla caviglia con taglio sotto il seno, realizzato in cady di seta. avorio. I due pannelli da cui è formato si uniscono sul davanti e sul dietro. La cucitura posteriore si apre a formare uno spacco a partire dall’incavo del ginocchio a terra e nella parte superiore è inserita la chiusura-lampo. L’abito è foderato in crêpe de chine avorio. Cabane di linea svasata con maniche a kimono, lungo al secondo fianco. L’ampiezza delle maniche è raccolta al fondo formando pieghe trattenute da un polsino con chiusura ad asola e bottone ricoperto. Il capo presenta in ogni sua parte un complesso ricamo in fili dorati e viscosa a volute barocche intrecciate tra loro, arricchite da motivi floreali. L’orlo irregolare a smerli segue l’andamento delle volute. La chiusura frontale è assicurata da un bottone automatico ricoperto.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.label: Gianfranco Ferré Couture (scritta oro)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.exhibitions:
Gianfranco Ferré e Maria Luigia: Inattese Assonanze”, Palazzo del Governatore, Parma, 2016; “Gianfranco Ferré. Moda, un
racconto nei disegni”, Centro Culturale Santa Maria della Pietà, Cremona, 2017;
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.working_process: Sovrapposizione di tessuti (tulle, nylon e lurex) uniti tra loro da ricamo a mano con impunture parallele in filo lurex spiralato; ricamo a mano a volute e fiori con effetto rilievo

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: An exquisitely simple black evening dress with asymmetric flaring toward bottom finds an element of distinction in the torchon effect that gives definition to an empire line. By contrast, the sumptuous little jacket bordering on a bolero sparks images of a precious English Tudor or Spanish jerkin. It’s all about the spirals reminiscent of the famous Thonet Style (wood chairs) that become all the more luxurious thanks to the chunky flower decors, inserts & ringlets in a variety of materials. Nicely offsetting this quasi-baroque impact is the transparency in back.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Dress in layers of silk georgette. Empire style, long trumpet skirt. Torchon crossover band at bustline setting in motion deep unpressed pleat all the way down to asymmetric hem: ankle skimming in front, with small train in back. Bolero with elaborate gold thread openwork/spiral embroidery in front. Perfectly symmetric lapels. Semi sheer mid back thanks to layers of tulle & mesh topstitched together with a delicate gold thread. At back neck and base of bolero two pairs of ringlets. Small silk flowers on shoulders, short sleeves and part of front.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.exhibitions:
“Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015; “Gianfranco Ferré. Moda, un racconto nei
disegni”, Centro Culturale Santa Maria della Pietà, Cremona, 2017;
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.working_process: Hand application of silk flowers; spiral thread embroidery/topstitching (by hand) on tulle, nylon and lurex, so joining the three fabrics in layers; hand embroidery in raffia, complete with relief effect; hand embroidery (in openwork pattern) of gold lurex spiral thread.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.materials: Black silk georgette (Clerici, Como; art. 1031, col. 9), beige silk organza (Clerici, Como), raffia, gold lurex diagonal mesh, stiff white nylon tulle, gold lurex spiral thread, iridescent beige silk taffeta

L’orlo irregolare a smerli segue l’andamento delle volute. La chiusura frontale è assicurata da un bottone automatico ricoperto.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: An intentional trompe l’oeil effect defines the heart & soul of this evening dress. The bodice is actually a wide bustier-belt mimicking an exquisite piece of sculpture or a supremely feminine type of armor with beautifully swirling basque. This fabulous creation – complete with amazing floral decoration – complements an airy light and absolutely fluid silk taffeta skirt. This is the second prototype. The original runway one is on display at the Pitti Palace Costume Gallery in Florence (donation in 2000).
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Two-piece evening dress in sandy beige silk taffeta. Slim whalebone bodice with wide band along strapless neckline all about embroidery and other lavish ornamentation (gold sequins, gold metallic mesh, gold beads, organza flowers, raffia spikelets). Back zip closure. Long straight skirt with back slit. Bustier-belt in tulle, raffia and gold metallic mesh with rich embroidery & beading (fine gold silk cord, gold and wood beads). Wide swingy basque, spiral edging. Back snap closure. Shawl accessory.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: An exquisitely simple black evening dress with asymmetric flaring toward bottom finds an element of distinction in the torchon effect that gives definition to an empire line. By contrast, the sumptuous little jacket bordering on a bolero sparks images of a precious English Tudor or Spanish jerkin. It’s all about the spirals reminiscent of the famous Thonet Style (wood chairs) that become all the more luxurious thanks to the chunky flower decors, inserts & ringlets in a variety of materials. Nicely offsetting this quasi-baroque impact is the transparency in back.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Dress in layers of silk georgette. Empire style, long trumpet skirt. Torchon crossover band at bustline setting in motion deep unpressed pleat all the way down to asymmetric hem: ankle skimming in front, with small train in back. Bolero with elaborate gold thread openwork/spiral embroidery in front. Perfectly symmetric lapels. Semi sheer mid back thanks to layers of tulle & mesh topstitched together with a delicate gold thread. At back neck and base of bolero two pairs of ringlets. Small silk flowers on shoulders, short sleeves and part of front.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.exhibitions:
“Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015; “Gianfranco Ferré. Moda, un racconto nei
disegni”, Centro Culturale Santa Maria della Pietà, Cremona, 2017;
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.working_process: Hand application of silk flowers; spiral thread embroidery/topstitching (by hand) on tulle, nylon and lurex, so joining the three fabrics in layers; hand embroidery in raffia, complete with relief effect; hand embroidery (in openwork pattern) of gold lurex spiral thread.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.materials: Black silk georgette (Clerici, Como; art. 1031, col. 9), beige silk organza (Clerici, Como), raffia, gold lurex diagonal mesh, stiff white nylon tulle, gold lurex spiral thread, iridescent beige silk taffeta

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: An exquisitely simple black evening dress with asymmetric flaring toward bottom finds an element of distinction in the torchon effect that gives definition to an empire line. By contrast, the sumptuous little jacket bordering on a bolero sparks images of a precious English Tudor or Spanish jerkin. It’s all about the spirals reminiscent of the famous Thonet Style (wood chairs) that become all the more luxurious thanks to the chunky flower decors, inserts & ringlets in a variety of materials. Nicely offsetting this quasi-baroque impact is the transparency in back.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Dress in layers of silk georgette. Empire style, long trumpet skirt. Torchon crossover band at bustline setting in motion deep unpressed pleat all the way down to asymmetric hem: ankle skimming in front, with small train in back. Bolero with elaborate gold thread openwork/spiral embroidery in front. Perfectly symmetric lapels. Semi sheer mid back thanks to layers of tulle & mesh topstitched together with a delicate gold thread. At back neck and base of bolero two pairs of ringlets. Small silk flowers on shoulders, short sleeves and part of front.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: The actual dress, for the most part a short simple sheath, comes gloriously to life thanks to the big floral decors on the bodice. They create the impression of a magical garden as rich and luxurious as the long flowing detachable skirt open in front. What makes the latter so sumptuous is the array of layers & flounces that spark images of 19th century petticoats. Meanwhile, although usually the utmost expression of severity elegance, here black accentuates the sense of opulence. Also by capturing glints of light.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.present_location: open care
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Evening ensemble in two pieces: Short bustier style dress with slim line, above-the-knee length. Big flowers in lacquered silk all over the bodice. Floor-length skirt, ¾ circular style, tied onto the dress with a topstitched silk ribbon. Base part in three layers of tulle topstitched together, then covered lavishly with mesh & point d’esprit veil flounces. Silk organza lining.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.working_process: Application of handmade flowers in lacquered silk and organza; 1 cm parallel toptitching on tulle and veil, so joining the materials in layers; point d’esprit veil and mesh ruffles in horizontal parallel flounce form
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.materials: Wool/silk (Taroni, Como; art. 17055), point d’ésprit veil (Solstiss, Paris; art. 225026), mesh (Rodolph Simon, Paris; art. 53229), horsehair ribbon, nylon tulle, silk organza (Clerici Tessuto, Como)

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.materials: Wool/silk (Taroni, Como; art. 17055), point d’ésprit veil (Solstiss, Paris; art. 225026), mesh (Rodolph Simon, Paris; art. 53229), horsehair ribbon, nylon tulle, silk organza (Clerici Tessuto, Como)

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.bibliography: “Gianfranco Ferré. I quaderni di Pitti 01”, Giunti Editore, Firenze, 2000
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.remark: Despite the simple, straightforward image of this contemporary low-cut top, the construction process is definitely complex. Whatever, all the visual, material & decorative opulence is in the bottom part of the dress, both in the short slim (albeit ultra precious) underskirt and in the sumptuous flounce skirt so wonderfully reminiscent of 19th century petticoats. Complete with rich embroidery & ruffles, the primary skirt finds a further note of novelty in the reinvention of nylon mesh in the form of woven Vienna straw. Donation to the Pitti Palace Costume Gallery in Florence (2000)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.description: Evening dress in three pieces: Bodice in black silk gros grain. Construction in seven small panels diminishing in size from center outward. Whalebone at seams. Topstitched black faille waistband. Back zip closure. Flounce skirt open in front. In ecru color tulle. Doubling in ivory lace with floral embroidery. Faux-raffia nylon ruffles. Ecru silk organza lining. Underskirt in ecru silk crepe with doubling in lace (same as skirt). Knee length with semi scallop-edge hem. Side zip closure. Organza rose decor.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1989_ss.exhibitions: “Altre emozioni”, Palazzo Pitti, Firenze, 2000; “Moda tra analogie e dissonanze”, Firenze, 2010
